The 2026 Oscar® Winner for Best Animated Short Film from the acclaimed team of Chris Lavis and Maciek Szczerbowski. events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: "On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​" Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay

events.title: Blackout Poetry: Reading Between The Lines
events.description: Blackout poetry is a fresh form of ‘creative destruction’, where instead of starting a poem with a blank page, you take on a page full of words and reduce it to create something new and unique! Buttons made using images of items from the Baldwin Collection of Canadiana. Left: Photograph of Toronto Public Library, Mechanics' Institute building (1890). Right: Map plate from Ortelius' atlas Theatrvm orbis terrarvm (published 1570).

events.title: Let's Learn About Computer Hardware
events.description: This hands-on workshop series introduces the process of building a gaming desktop, from selecting components to installing an operating system. The series also covers OS selection and installation, along with essential cybersecurity tips using open-source tools. This workshop is run by Free Geek Toronto.

events.title: Solar Observing Party

events.title: Youth Hub: Sushi Making with Chef Sang Kim
events.description: Join us for a delicious Sushi Making workshop presented by TV host and celebrity chef, Sang Kim, who will teach you the basics of sushi-making. Participants will make sushi rolls using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of sushi, its origins and eating etiquette will also be shared. Food allergy notice: Please be aware that food will be used in this program. Certain food ingredients may cause allergic reactions in some people. This is a Youth Hub progra

events.title: Volunteer Toronto: High School Volunteering - AMA
events.description: This workshop focuses conversations around high school volunteering. It discusses volunteer requirements for school, how to collect hours, where to find volunteer roles, how to apply, and what to expect after. Ages 13-17. Registration required. Presented by Volunteer Toronto . events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service.
